Scenario.title: Night Dragon Targeting Energy Sector via Phishing
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple A basic-skilled threat group named Night Dragon initiates a targeted phishing campaign against energy sector organizations, leveraging malicious image payloads and automated exfiltration techniques to compromise systems and steal operational data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Application Log Content, Command Execution

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2022-23134 (Suggested): Malicious actors can pass step checks and potentially change the configuration of Zabbix Frontend.
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails containing malicious attachments to energy sector employees, exploiting human error to establish initial access. The compromised Zabbix Frontend configuration allows attackers to bypass authentication mechanisms and deploy payloads.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-28205 (Suggested): Apple iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and Safari WebKit contain a use-after-free vulnerability that leads to code execution when processing maliciously crafted web content.
stage.description: The attacker deploys malicious image files embedded in application logs, exploiting the WebKit vulnerability to execute arbitrary code. This enables remote code execution on affected systems, allowing the deployment of persistent backdoors.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker uses command-line tools to automate the exfiltration of sensitive operational data. Encrypted communication channels mask data transfers, ensuring stealthy extraction of critical energy infrastructure information from compromised systems.

Scenario.summary: Night Dragon leverages phishing, malicious image exploitation, and automated exfiltration to compromise energy sector networks. The attack chain begins with deceptive emails, progresses to code execution via vulnerable applications, and culminates in covert data theft using encrypted command channels.
